key_insight: A symmetric dual-chip piezoelectric strain-cell design can deliver homogeneous uniaxial strain to thick, square semiconductor quantum-device substrates while minimizing flexure and shear.
engineering_relevance: The work enables practical strain tuning of quantum devices by combining high-density RF/DC wiring, electrical isolation from high-voltage actuators, and a form factor compatible with standard chip bonding.
method: Use a highly symmetric dual-chip loading configuration to suppress bending and shear.
Integrate a high-density RF/DC interposer for dense qubit wiring.
Enclose piezo actuators in a grounded Faraday cage to reduce unwanted electric-field-induced shifts.
evidence_signal: Finite element simulations and surface strain measurements on a 200 μm-thick silicon die showed 215 μϵ strain at 200 V applied piezo bias.
watch_item: Performance depends on precise symmetric mounting and robust electrical isolation, which may be challenging to reproduce in tightly packaged cryogenic hardware.
paper_title: A Cryogenic Uniaxial Strain Cell for Quantum Devices
